title: Venera 9: Venus's First Orbiting Visitor
story: Zooming through space, a Soviet spacecraft named Venera 9 made history in 1975. On October 20, it became the first human-made object to orbit Venus, our mysterious neighbor planet. Venera means 'Venus' in Russian. This pioneering mission gave scientists a closer look at the cloudy world, helping unlock some of its secrets. It paved the way for future explorations of our solar system's planets.
activity_question: What was the name of the spacecraft that first orbited Venus?
bonus_fact: Venus is often called Earth's twin because they're similar in size and mass.

story: Swords clashed and armies collided in a massive battle that changed Japan forever. On October 20, 1600, the Battle of Sekigahara erupted. Two powerful clans fought for control, but the Tokugawa emerged victorious. This epic showdown set the stage for the Tokugawa clan to become Japan's rulers, known as shoguns, ushering in a new era of Japanese history.
activity_question: What title did the Tokugawa clan gain after winning the battle?
follow_up_question: Can you imagine how life in Japan might have changed under new rulers?

title: Flames Engulf Copenhagen
story: Flames leapt from building to building as a massive fire swept through Copenhagen in 1728. Starting at the West Gate on October 20, the blaze raged for three days. It devoured about a third of Denmark's capital city before finally being extinguished. Despite the destruction, the resilient people of Copenhagen banded together to rebuild their beloved city.
activity_question: Where did the Great Fire of Copenhagen start?
follow_up_question: How do you think the people of Copenhagen felt after the fire was finally put out?

title: A Massive Land Deal Changes America
story: Imagine doubling the size of your country overnight! That's what happened when the US Senate approved the Louisiana Purchase on October 20, 1803. This huge land deal with France added a vast territory to America, stretching from the Mississippi River to the Rocky Mountains. It opened up new frontiers for exploration and settlement, shaping the nation's future in a big way.
activity_question: What did the Louisiana Purchase add to America?
follow_up_question: Can you name any states that were part of the Louisiana Purchase?
